channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New book alleges that someone at Fox News gave Trump's team early look at town hall questions
item.description: Someone inside Fox News gave Donald Trump's presidential campaign the questions he would face ahead of a town hall the network produced in January 2024, a forthcoming book alleges, according to excerpts reported by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about wildfires raining embers onto the Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — At least five people were killed and more than 1,000 structures were destroyed as fierce wildfires raged in the Los Angeles area, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Reporter douses flames endangering California home on live TV
item.description: While covering one of the multiple fires currently ablaze in California, one reporter — a former EMT and volunteer firefighter — stepped in to stop flames from spreading to a home.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fire hydrants ran dry in California, highlighting a major problem in firefighting
item.description: For some 15 hours as wildfires spiraled out of control in Los Angeles, the public water system faced four times its usual demand, causing some hydrants to run dry and hindering the fight against the flames, local water officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Guantanamo's prisoners have reached a pivotal moment. So has the 9/11 case. Here's what to know
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After 23 years, the fate of the last remaining Guantanamo detainees swept up worldwide after al-Qaida’s shattering Sept. 11, 2001, attacks is reaching a pivotal moment this month. Court battles and dealmaking are deciding the future of many of those last men at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Stowaways on planes and inside landing gear raise worries about aviation security
item.description: What is going on with aviation security? People have been found dead hiding in the wheel wells of planes twice in the past month. Two stowaways were arrested on different flights in November and December. Then a passenger opened an emergency door while a plane was taxiing in Boston Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US troops need to stay in Syria to counter the Islamic State group, Austin says
item.description: RAMSTEIN AIR BASE, Germany (AP) — The U.S. needs to keep troops deployed in Syria to prevent the Islamic State group from reconstituting as a major threat following the ouster of Bashar Assad's government, Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Venezuela's ultimate political survivor Nicolás Maduro faces his toughest challenge yet
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro is facing more international rebuke than at any time in his 12 years in power. The self-declared socialist is widely believed to have lost last year's election by a landslide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jimmy Carter's life intersected with slavery's legacy. His record on Civil Rights is complicated
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Young Jimmy Carter and his friends were walking across a pasture after a day’s farm labor during the Great Depression. As they came to a gate, his companions stood aside and let Carter enter first. This was no act of kindness or intuitive deference to a future U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The polar vortex readies to dump snow on Texas and its neighbors
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — An area stretching from Texas to Tennessee braced Wednesday for the expected arrival of freezing rain and snow, as some other parts of the country that already received an arctic blast this week prepared to go another round with the plunging polar vortex.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump blames Newsom for California's raging wildfires
item.description: The wildfires that started on Tuesday have killed two people and destroyed more than 1,000 structures.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg jumps into action as brush fire burns in Pacific Palisades
item.description: The "Police Academy" actor was among those working to move abandoned vehicles blocking emergency crews responding to the blaze.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Four San Diego State fraternity members charged after one set on fire: DA
item.description: The incident involving the Phi Kappa Psi members occurred last February, when the fraternity was on probation with the university due to issues of hazing .
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, NewsNation

item.title: Wildfires latest: 2 people killed and over 1,000 structures destroyed, per LA fire chief
item.description: Fierce wildfires are raging in the Los Angeles area, fueled by powerful Santa Ana winds, sending residents fleeing from burning homes through flames, ferocious winds and towering clouds of smoke.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 4 fraternity members at San Diego State University charged after one was set on fire during a 'skit': DA
item.description: The incident occurred last February, when the fraternity was on probation with the university over issues of hazing and conduct that threatens the health and safety of students.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KRON4

item.title: Justice Department says it plans to release only part of special counsel's Trump report for now
item.description: The Justice Department says it will release special counsel Jack Smith’s findings on Donald Trump’s efforts to undo the results of the 2020 presidential election but will keep the rest of the report under wraps for now.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: President Joe Biden said early Wednesday that he thinks he would have beaten President-elect Donald Trump if he stuck with his reelection bid in an interview with USA Today.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Mailman's family breaks silence after father of 2 killed on lunch break
item.description: Manhattan 24-year-old Jaia Cruz charged with murder in the stabbing death of U.S. Postal worker Ray Hodge III after a fight in the line at a Harlem deli.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean court clears government, adoption agency of liability in adoptee's deportation from US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court on Wednesday cleared the government and an adoption agency of all liability in a lawsuit filed by a 49-year-old Korean man whose traumatic adoption journey led to an abusive childhood in the United States and ultimately his deportation to South Korea in
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fierce overnight winds expected to fuel Pacific Palisades fire
item.description: “We looked across and the fire had jumped from one side of the road to the other side of the road,” Resident Kelsey Trainor said. “People were getting out of the cars with their dogs and babies and bags, they were crying and screaming. The road was just blocked, like full-on blocked for an hour.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California governor says Pacific Palisades wildfire has destroyed many structures as winds kick up
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A wildfire whipped up by extreme winds swept through a Los Angeles hillside dotted with celebrity residences Tuesday, burning homes in Pacific Palisades and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JANIE HAR and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fierce overnight winds expected to fuel Pacific Palisades fire
item.description: “We looked across and the fire had jumped from one side of the road to the other side of the road,” Resident Kelsey Trainor said. “People were getting out of the cars with their dogs and babies and bags, they were crying and screaming. The road was just blocked, like full-on blocked for an hour.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wildfire burns through LA enclave known for its beaches and celebrity allure
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rimmed to the south by a 3-mile stretch of Pacific beach, and to the north by craggy bluffs and deep, verdant canyons, the seaside enclave of Pacific Palisades has long been a celebrity hideaway and magnet for those drawn to nature’s doorstep not far from the skyline of downtown L
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cybertruck driver used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas attack, police say
item.description: Police found a 6-page manifesto on Matthew Livelsberger’s phone and say he used ChatGPT to plan his New Year's Day bombing at the front of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas, the LVMPD said at a news conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Feds release new photo of New Orleans terrorist Shamsud-Din Jabbar as FBI seeks more tips
item.description: FBI releases image showing New Orleans terrorist Shamsud-Din Jabbar biking through the French Quarter on Halloween, months before attacking New Year's revelers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These will be the most competitive housing markets in 2025, Zillow report predicts
item.description: The hottest housing markets this year are predicted to be on the East Coast, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: New York appeals court denies halting Trump's sentencing
item.description: A New York appellate judge on Tuesday declined to suspend President-elect Trump’s Friday sentencing on his hush money criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tibet earthquake kills at least 126 people and leaves many trapped
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble and killing at least 126 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Court proceedings for man charged in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing are delayed several weeks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of gunning down UnitedHealthcare's CEO outside a New York City hotel is unlikely to appear in Manhattan federal court again before mid-February after prosecutors and lawyers on Monday requested more time to prepare the case for trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Flight cancellations pile up as major winter storm hits East Coast
item.description: Flight cancellations and delays are stacking up as a major winter storm makes its way across the East Coast.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau resigns
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Detachment risk prompts recall of 145K trailer hitch locks
item.description: The recall says the vehicle may become detached from whatever it's towing due to the potentially loose part on the hitch locks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Lions beat Vikings 31-9, win NFC North and No. 1 seed, dropping division rivals to No. 5
item.description: Jahmyr Gibbs was the game-breaking player the Detroit Lions needed to get their high-powered offense in gear — and give them a first-round bye and home-field advantage in the NFC playoffs.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lawsuit alleges Fox Sports ex-host harassed hairstylist and offered her $1.5M for sex
item.description: A woman who worked as a hairstylist for Fox Sports alleges in a lawsuit that former host Skip Bayless made repeated, unwanted advances toward her — including an offer of $1.5 million to have sex with him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Royal family pays tribute to Prince William, Harry's nanny who lost stepson in New Orleans attack
item.description: Prince William shared his condolences to the family of his former nanny after her stepson was killed in the New Year’s terror attack in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WGN 9

item.title: Selena's killer, Yolanda Saldívar, files for parole, begins review process
item.description: Saldívar, now 64 years-old, was sentenced to life in prison on Oct. 26, 1995, with the possibility of parole after 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Mendez, WGN 9
